title: Get Data Stream Context
content:
# Get Data Stream Context

Retrieve an array of Assets and respective data sources contextualized within a specific Data Stream.

## Endpoint

`GET /datastreams/{datastream_name}/context/get`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.datastreams.getDataStreamContext()
print(response)

parameters.name: datastream_name
parameters.description: Data Stream key `name` to get. The string can only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters and `.`, `_` or `-` characters.

context.description: An array of objects associated with the Data Stream. Each object contains keys for the Asset `name` of the Asset / Data Stream pair and and the Source for the pair.

created.description: UTC time when the Asset / Data Stream pair was first created, formatted in RFC 3339.

resource.description: Asset `name` that is associated with the Data Stream.

source.description: Workload `name` that is sending data to the Asset / Data Stream pair.

updated.description: UTC time when any Asset / Data Stream pair keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.

writable.description: Indicates whether the Asset / Data Stream pair `write` key is read/write (`true`) or read only (`false`).

created.description: UTC time when the Data Stream was first created, formatted in RFC 3339.

datastream_name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Data Stream.

updated.description: UTC time when any Data Stream keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.
